reasoning: The order mandates permanent physical injury based on a physiological myth ('callousing ears') and explicitly contradicts military safety doctrine. No reasonable person would defend a commander ordering soldiers to sustain permanent hearing loss for 'toughness,' as this objectively degrades combat readiness.

The rule mandates compliance with a condition (possessing a specific decal) that is factually impossible to fulfill because the issuing infrastructure no longer exists. No reasonable person would defend enforcing a requirement that the authority itself has made impossible to satisfy.
